title: coreopsis capillacea
intro: Coreopsis capillacea, also known as Tickseed, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow blooms and hairy stems. Traditionally, Coreopsis capillacea has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, making it a potential remedy for wound healing, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis capillacea can be found in tea, tincture, and capsule forms, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
